info.title: DevStation Core — Executions
info.description: Long-running executions infrastructure. Follows Google AIP-151 conventions: executions are caller-issued units of work with best-effort cancellation and an attachable progress stream. Streaming is delivered via `execution.event` JSON-RPC notifications (no `id`) emitted during an `execution.watch` request — the same channel carries request/response and notifications, demuxed by frame shape (LSP / MCP convention).

methods.name: execution.watch
methods.description: Subscribes to an operation's event stream. The server emits `execution.event` notifications for every event produced so far plus future events, until a terminal event closes the stream. The final response on this request id is an empty Ack — the terminal arrived as a notification.

methods.name: execution.cancel
methods.description: Best-effort cancellation of a running operation (AIP-151 semantics). The server signals cancellation to the underlying task; the terminal event observed by watchers may be `cancelled`, or `succeeded`/`failed` if the operation completed before the signal landed.

methods.name: execution.list
methods.description: Snapshot of every operation currently tracked. Useful for observability — listing in-flight and recently terminated operations for a monitoring screen.

ExecutionEvent.title: ExecutionEvent
ExecutionEvent.description: Tagged union of events emitted during an operation's lifetime. Discriminated by the `type` field. BCs may extend the union by declaring additional variants in their own schemas that follow the same shape (`type` literal + payload).

Succeeded.title: Succeeded
Succeeded.description: Terminal — operation completed without error. `result` carries an optional in-process payload (e.g. the installation list a installer task produces for the orchestrator); it is opaque on the wire — UI watchers ignore it and only react to the `succeeded` tag.

Failed.title: Failed
Failed.description: Terminal — operation completed with an error.

Cancelled.title: Cancelled
Cancelled.description: Terminal — operation was cancelled by an explicit execution.cancel.

ExecutionEventNotification.title: ExecutionEventNotification
ExecutionEventNotification.description: Params shape for the server-initiated `execution.event` notification.
